description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a pitched roof made of blue tiles. The well is topped with a wooden canopy supported by sturdy wooden beams, and features a large wooden wheel mechanism on one side, likely used for drawing water. A wooden bucket is visible both inside the well and resting on the ground nearby, suggesting its use for water retrieval. The stonework is weathered and textured, with some green moss or plant growth visible on the base, indicating exposure to the elements. A small lantern hangs from one side of the roof, providing illumination. The well sits on a roughly octagonal stone platform with patches of grass and earth around its perimeter.
description: A circular stone water well with a blue-tiled roof, wooden wheel mechanism, and bucket, set on a mossy stone platform with a hanging lantern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-tiled roof, open well mouth, and a wooden bucket on the ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden support beams, a central wooden axle, and a hanging lantern.
description_view_2: Rear view with the blue-tiled roof, open well, and lantern visible, but no wheel mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view featuring the large wooden wheel mechanism and a bucket on the ground.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled roof made of blue tiles supported by wooden beams; no solid cover over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Large wooden wheel mechanism with a rope and bucket, placed on the side of the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high, with a wooden canopy roof supported by beams.
